summary: Dependency-confusion squat with postinstall exfiltration of CI environment metadata
details: @businessapp-microsites/apis@9999.0.1 squats an unregistered npm scope using version 9999.0.1, a canonical max-version chosen to outrank any internal package of the same name. Its package.json declares a postinstall script that runs an inline `node -e` program which issues an HTTPS GET to poc-trustpilot-npm-1782770591.testingboxes.com, transmitting installer CI/identity environment variables (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, GITHUB_SHA, GITHUB_REF, GITHUB_WORKFLOW, RUNNER_NAME, CI, GITHUB_ACTIONS, npm_config_user_agent) as query parameters along with a unique callback token. The package self-describes as a Bugcrowd dependency-confusion PoC, but the install hook fires on any installer that happens to resolve this scope, egressing their CI metadata to a third-party host they did not opt into. The hook runs automatically on `npm install` with no user interaction.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
